description_long: This object is a decorative string of triangular pennants, commonly used for festive or celebratory purposes in urban environments such as parks, streets, or event spaces. The pennants are arranged in an alternating color pattern of red and blue, each triangle hanging point-down from a thin horizontal cord or string. The triangles are evenly spaced and appear to be made of lightweight fabric or plastic, suitable for outdoor use. The cord is taut and straight, suggesting it is designed to be suspended between two fixed points, such as poles or walls. The overall construction is simple, with no visible text, logos, or additional embellishments. The pennants are in excellent condition, with crisp edges and vibrant colors.
description: A string of alternating red and blue triangular pennants hanging from a straight cord, designed for festive urban decoration. The pennants are evenly spaced and in pristine condition.
description_view_0: Front view showing four triangular pennants, alternating red and blue, hanging from a horizontal cord.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the cord and pennants, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same alternating red and blue triangles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge of the cord and pennants.
attribute_mounting_location: between poles or across open spaces
attribute_graphic_design: Alternating solid red and blue triangular pennants with no text or symbols. The triangles are evenly spaced along a straight brown cord.
